name: array_grid_numX3_offX2
description: Reference-captured parity case for the Array tool (task 0355, mesh.arrayTool), backed by the new 3-axis grid kernel Mesh.arrayFacesGrid. Frozen from the toolcard's Stage-1 fuzz case (vibe3d_private/toolcards/poly.array/findings.md §6): a plain 1x1x1 unit cube, all 6 faces selected (matches the reference's default Source=Active Meshes), numX=3/offX=2 with every other Array Generator/Clone Effector attr left at its captured live default (numY=numZ=1, offY=offZ=0, Jitter=0, Scale=100%, Rotate=0, Between=off, Replace Source=off, Invert Polygons=off, Merge Vertices=off/dist=0). The reference's own documented semantics (Offset = per-step spacing between source and first clone; Scale/Rotate/Jitter at neutral defaults leave every clone byte-identical to the source, just translated) predict exactly 24 vertices (8 original + 2x8 clones) / 18 faces (6x3), clone k translated by (2*k, 0, 0) -- this is a same-shape parity anchor with vibe3d's PRE-existing 1D-line arrayFaces (numY=numZ=1 degenerates the grid to a line), not yet a genuine multi-axis capture (see findings.md §6 note on a follow-on 2-axis case). The golden vertex list below was pulled from a live vibe3d run of this exact recipe and independently checked against the reference's own translate-by-(2k,0,0) rule before freezing -- no reference engine runs at test time.
source: Frozen from vibe3d_private/toolcards/poly.array/findings.md §6 (captured parity case), replayed once against vibe3d's tool.set/tool.attr/tool.doApply headless path and cross-checked against the reference's documented Offset/Scale/Rotate semantics before freezing as golden.
